Document route

Confidential Payout Evidence Packet

Reviewer-visible packet for private payout and treasury motion corridors showing how confidentiality, governance, and trust surfaces already connect on Devnet.

Document context

Evidence packet only; it does not claim production-ready real-funds payout or completed external audit and receipt requirements.

Audience: Judges, grant reviewers, buyers, operators

Try in productOpen verification routeOpen generated proof
Open raw file

Confidential Payout Evidence Packet

Purpose

This packet exists for the confidential-governance and privacy-commercial corridors inside PrivateDAO.

It answers one practical reviewer question:

Can PrivateDAO already connect private governance to a believable confidential payout workflow?

Short answer

Yes, on Devnet and inside the current evidence boundary.

PrivateDAO already supports:

  • private governance framing
  • commit-reveal voting
  • confidential treasury motion rehearsal
  • reviewer-visible trust and custody packets
  • security and privacy surfaces that explain why confidentiality matters operationally

It does not yet claim:

  • real-funds production payout readiness
  • closed source-verifiable settlement receipts
  • external audit completion

What works now

Governance to treasury corridor

The product can already tell a coherent operational story:

  1. create DAO
  2. create proposal
  3. private vote
  4. finalize decision
  5. execute governed treasury motion

Reviewer-visible payout context

  • security route: /security
  • services route: /services
  • custody route: /custody
  • grant committee pack: /documents/grant-committee-pack
  • launch trust packet: /documents/launch-trust-packet

Why this matters commercially

Confidential payout is not just a privacy demo.

It maps directly to:

  • payroll approvals
  • contributor disbursements
  • grant committee releases
  • high-sensitivity treasury motions

The product value is the combination of:

  • confidentiality
  • governance legitimacy
  • reviewer-visible trust boundaries

Strongest reviewer path

  1. Open /security
  2. Open /services
  3. Open /custody
  4. Open /documents/grant-committee-pack
  5. Open /documents/launch-trust-packet

What judges should see

  • a practical confidential payout use case
  • reviewer-visible explanation of where privacy sits in the workflow
  • clear release-boundary visibility before any production or real-funds claim

Current release boundary

This packet proves confidential payout rehearsal and reviewer-visible evidence on Devnet.

It does not prove:

  • production payout custody
  • production-ready confidential settlement
  • closed external audit and receipt requirements

Public-good value

This packet helps the ecosystem because it makes:

  • private-yet-reviewable payout flows easier to understand
  • governance-linked payout discipline easier to inspect
  • complex privacy and cryptography behavior easier to verify from a product shell instead of a research memo

Canonical linked docs

  • docs/grant-committee-pack.md
  • docs/launch-trust-packet.generated.md
  • docs/mainnet-blockers.md
  • docs/cryptographic-confidence-engine.md
  • docs/zk-capability-matrix.md
  • docs/refhe-security-model.md
  • docs/frontier-integrations.generated.md

PrivateDAO is confidential coordination infrastructure for organizations on Solana, connecting private operations to verifiable outcomes.
Developed in public through github.com/X-PACT/PrivateDAO with product proof, runtime evidence, and final security hardening moving in parallel.

Brand, product design, documentation, digital evidence, and official deployments are reserved; official-looking derivative deployments, service packaging, or commercial use require written coordination through official PrivateDAO channels.
